content: Curiosity-driven deep active agents can adopt a dynamic 'uncertainty gatekeeping' mechanism where only memory samples that exceed a context-dependent uncertainty threshold are retained for future learning, rather than selecting a fixed number of high-uncertainty samples per training cycle. This mechanism ties intrinsic curiosity reward directly to memory compression efficiency, aligning what the agent finds surprising with what it chooses to remember long-term.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
trigger_topic: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
